name: Kaneohe
desc: Budget-friendlier windward alternative to Kailua — bay views, lush mountains, no beach but stunning scenery.
insider: Kaneohe Bay sandbar appears at low tide — rent a boat or join a tour to get there, it's one of Oahu's best-kept secrets
Windward Mall is old-school but has the only movie theater on the windward side — Consolidated Theatres
Valley of the Temples Memorial Park has the stunning Byodo-In Temple replica — a peaceful, photogenic cultural site
The Haiku Stairs (Stairway to Heaven) are officially closed and fenced off — the city voted to remove them
Haleiwa Joe's in Haiku Gardens serves excellent seafood and steaks in a setting surrounded by lush Ko'olau peaks
Kaneohe is significantly cheaper than Kailua for similar homes — locals call it the smart windward value play
He'eia Pier General Store & Deli has solid poke bowls and plate lunches right on the water — locals love this spot
The H-3 freeway to Pearl Harbor is one of the most scenic highways in America — and it's your daily commute
Ho'omaluhia Botanical Garden is free, stunning, and has camping — the Ko'olau mountain backdrop is breathtaking
Rain is heavier here than Kailua — Kaneohe averages 60+ inches per year, so mold prevention is a real concern for homeowners
